title: astragalus caucasicus
intro: Astragalus caucasicus, also known as Caucasian milkvetch, is a perennial herb belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the Caucasus region. Its roots and rhizomes have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which help to boost the body's natural defenses and protect against stress. The health benefits of Astragalus caucasicus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Astragalus caucasicus can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
